title: Introduction to Solutions
subject: Chemistry
topic: Introduction to Solutions and their Classification
key_points: Manzil series focuses on theoretical discussion and PYQs for JEE Main preparation.
Advice to practice PYQs from the last 4-6 years due to NTA's predictable nature.
First Manzil test is scheduled for November 30th.
A Solution is defined as a homogeneous mixture of two or more non-reacting substances.
Homogeneous means uniform throughout the system.
Heterogeneous mixtures (e.g., sand in water) are not considered solutions.
Solutions are classified by the number of components: Binary (2 components), Ternary (3 components), Quaternary (4 components), etc.
The JEE syllabus primarily focuses on Binary Solutions.
Solute and Solvent can be any state of matter (solid, liquid, or gas), and the resulting solution can also be solid, liquid, or gas.
Common misconception that solutions are always liquid with solid solutes is incorrect.
next_topic: Concentration Terms, Vapor Pressure, and Colligative Properties

title: Chemical Bonding
subject: Chemistry
key_points: Chemical Bonding is identified as the most important chapter in chemistry, potentially contributing three questions in exams.
The lecture will focus on logic, reasoning, and question-attempting strategies.
The approximate duration of the chapter is 9 hours.
PW app features for the 'Maanjil' series include Class Notes, Daily Practice Problems (DPPs), Test Series (with a specific test on Nov 30th), PYQ sessions, Short Notes, and AI doubt assistance.
Enrollment for the course is completely free.

Key topics to be covered include: Valence Electron, Lewis Symbol, Octet Rule, Covalency, Valence Bond Theory (VBT), Resonance, Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR) Theory, Hybridization, pπ-pπ & pπ-dπ bonds, effects of percentage S-character on bond angle and bond length, Dipole Moment, Molecular Orbital Theory (MOT), Ionic Bonding (including Fajan's Rule), Intermolecular Forces of Attraction, and Hydrogen Bonding.
Topics such as Back Bonding, Bridge Bonding, Order of Boiling Point, and Bond Dissociation Energy will be covered in the P-block chapter.
The course aims to cover a total of 120 questions.
Recommended books are 'Easy Notes for Inorganic Chemistry' (for JEE) and 'NEET Easy Notes' (for NEET).
A Telegram channel will provide additional questions and some book PDFs.
next_topic: Group 1, 2, 13, 14, 15, 16, 17, 18 elements and their valence electrons.

title: JEE 2025: Relation & Function L1
subject: Mathematics
key_points: Introduction to JEE 2025 Lecture 1 on Relation & Function for Class 12.
The session offers a detailed one-shot covering basics to advanced concepts, suitable for new learners.
Practice sheets with video solutions are provided, including important questions from previous year's lectures with time-stamped solutions.
Topics to be covered: Relation, Types of Relation, Function & its classification, Composition of Function, Inverse of a Function.
Identified 'Types of Relation' and 'Composition of Function' as critical topics for JEE Main & Advanced.
Revision of 11th-grade Relation concepts:
- Cartesian Product (A x B): definition, elements as ordered pairs, number of elements (|A| * |B|).
- Relation: defined as any subset of A x B. Total number of relations = 2^(|A x B|).
- Relation on A: refers to a relation from A to A (subset of A x A).
- Methods of describing a Relation: roster form (listing pairs), set-builder form (characteristic), 'a R b' notation.
- Understanding and identifying Domain, Codomain, and Range of a Relation through an example.
